chief_complaint: A 71-year-old female presented with severe febrile neutropenia and mild thrombocytopenia.
history: The patient has systemic lupus erythematosus and presented with M. pneumoniae pneumonia, which was complicated by severe neutropenia, mild thrombocytopenia, and erythema multiforme. Granulocyte autoantibodies were negative.
exam: Vital signs: fever, unknown temperature. Physical examination findings: unknown.
labs: Laboratory investigations: severe febrile neutropenia (0.2G/L), mild thrombocytopenia (75 G/L), erythema multiforme, granulocyte autoantibodies negative.
